stem: A healthcare study measures average patient recovery rate across 4 hospitals, each containing multiple departments. hospital A has 20 observations with mean 70.3 % (within-hospital SD = 9.8). hospital B has 5 observations with mean 45.3 % (within-hospital SD = 7.8). hospital C has 5 observations with mean 69.5 % (within-hospital SD = 9.4). hospital D has 15 observations with mean 62.8 % (within-hospital SD = 5.1). The between-hospital standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.5 and 13.8 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average patient recovery rate for hospital D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-hospital variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for hospital D: 62.8 % (shrunk from raw 62.8). Range [62.8, 62.9] as between-hospital SD varies from 5.5 to 13.8. Pooled mean: 64.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 64.9 % (weighted average of 4 hospital means).
Shrinkage factor for hospital D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=9.2: B = 0.98.
Interval [62.8, 62.9] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (62.8) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (64.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
